page: Amazon Web Services m5a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The instance provides 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated allocation model. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU hardware bundled with this instance, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data reveals that the m5a.2xlarge exhibits modest compute and memory performance. In single-core evaluations, such as stress-ng and Geekbench, performance falls into the bottom 10 percent, while multi-core metrics place it in the bottom 25 percent. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes consistently register in the bottom 25 percent. However, Passmark CPU and memory scores achieve average ratings, indicating stable performance for standard operations. Cryptographic tasks benefit from hardware-accelerated AES and SHA_NI instruction sets.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m5a.2xlarge offers a balanced resource profile for workloads that do not require high-performance compute or high-speed local storage. Given its 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average performance in standard database and text-generation benchmarks, this instance is suited for general-purpose applications, moderate web serving, and basic background processing tasks where cost optimization is prioritized over raw processing speed.
description: Amazon Web Services m5a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an AMD EPYC 7571 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. Managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 8 vCPUs across 4 physical cores and is equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 RAM. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate modest capabilities, with single-core and multi-core processing speeds generally ranking in the lower percentiles, though Passmark CPU and memory scores remain average. This instance represents a cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads, moderate web serving, and basic database operations where high-performance computing is not a primary requirement.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.2xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR4 RAM, and an AMD EPYC 7571 processor, offering a balanced, cost-efficient profile for standard workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5a.2xlarge is an AMD-powered general-purpose instance featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and DDR4 memory for cost-conscious standard workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 7571 processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores
Equipped with 32.0 GB of DDR4 system memory at 3 GHz
Delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps
Features dedicated CPU allocation managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Suited for general-purpose applications, moderate web serving, and basic database tasks
